message:
Overhaul UI streaming pipeline to eliminate flicker and improve responsiveness

The interactive TUI suffered from visible flicker during high-frequency
updates (spinner ticks, streaming LLM token deltas) because every event
triggered a full re-render. This commit introduces three coordinated
improvements:

1. **UiStreamDeltaBatcher** (agent.rs): Coalesces rapid text/thinking
   deltas into batched UI messages, flushing at 45ms intervals or when
   the buffer exceeds 2KB. Adjacent same-kind deltas are merged in-place
   to reduce message volume. Non-delta events (tool start/end, agent
   lifecycle) flush immediately to preserve ordering semantics.

2. **Lazy spinner initialization** (interactive.rs): Spinner tick chains
   are now started only on idle→busy transitions instead of at app init.
   Tick messages received while idle are silently dropped so old chains
   expire naturally. A new `show_processing_status_spinner()` predicate
   suppresses the generic "Processing..." row once visible stream
   progress (text or thinking deltas) is already being rendered, cutting
   redundant animated rows.

3. **Explicit Unicode-aware line wrapping** (view.rs): Streaming content
   and thinking lines are now hard-wrapped to the content area width
   using `UnicodeWidthChar` before being written, so the renderer's
   logical row count stays aligned with physical terminal rows. This
   prevents the off-by-one viewport jumps that caused scroll thrashing.
   Also fixes viewport height chrome accounting (header is 4 rows, not
   2) and adds `follow_stream_tail` support for auto-scrolling during
   active streaming.

- Adds unit tests for wrapped_plain_line edge cases

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
